discriminators: The SIZZLE of frying is the dominant real morphology: a dense, near-stationary high-frequency crackle texture — a very high onset_rate_per_second of micro-events, high high_band_energy, essentially no pitch, low crest. It is the same physical event as FIRE-SIZZLE (steam bursting out of oil) and signal analysis cannot separate the two; only the kitchen context in the name can.

Chopping is the other half of the subcategory: hard, roughly periodic impulses with a board resonance under them — a completely different morphology, which caps this at narrowable. Boiling is bubbling and will score toward WATER-BUBBLES.
A recording of the pan or pot itself being moved or set down is FOOD & DRINK-KITCHENWARE, not COOKING.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

discriminators: Ingestion, not liquid in free air. The liquid is inside a body, so everything is DARK, wet and damped: a gulp is a short low thump plus a throat click, and chugging is a periodic train of them at roughly one to three per second. Low spectral centroid with real low_band_energy is the cue.

FOOD & DRINK-POUR is the exact opposite — a bright, sustained, continuous liquid flow. The test is simple: if the liquid is falling into a vessel it is POUR, if it is going into a person or an animal it is DRINKING.
A slurp is noisy suction and will look like sustained noise; a swallow overlaps VOICES-EFFORTS. Animals lapping also live here, which pulls scoring toward the ANIMALS categories by name.

explanation: a dog eating or a horse eating. Swallowing

discriminators: Mastication. Short, broadband, granular, COMPLETELY damped impacts — a crunch is a burst of micro-fractures, so spectral_flatness is high and harmonicity is near zero — repeating at the roughly 2-4 Hz chewing rhythm, which gives it real onset_periodicity.

The absence of ring is the discriminator. Food and flesh have no decay time at all, unlike the ceramic and metal clinks of FOOD & DRINK-TABLEWARE, which ring audibly. A damped impulse train with zero decay is eating.
Wetter, darker and slower than the dry granular rattle of FOOD & DRINK-INGREDIENTS. Swallowing genuinely overlaps FOOD & DRINK-DRINKING and the two cannot be split on signal.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: specifically at a restaurant or bar. Isolated glassware recordings.

discriminators: The glass clink: an extremely fast attack, a very bright spectrum (most energy above 2 kHz, almost none below 200 Hz), a high crest_factor, and a clear ringing tail. Stemware rings noticeably longer than a thick tumbler, and a bottle rings lower than a wine glass.
This is signal-IDENTICAL to GLASS-IMPACT, GLASS-HANDLE and GLASS-MOVEMENT. The only thing placing it in FOOD & DRINK is the bar or restaurant context in the name.
Duller, darker clinks with a much shorter ring are ceramic or metal — FOOD & DRINK-TABLEWARE. Glass is the brightest and longest-ringing of the three tableware materials.

explanation: things like that. Raw food recordings often used for other design purposes

discriminators: Raw food material recorded as sound-design source. The only physics the whole subcategory shares is that food does not ring: everything here is broadband, damped and unpitched, with essentially zero decay time.

Beyond that it splits in two. Dry ingredients (rice, nuts, seeds, grain, cereal) read as a granular texture with a very high micro-onset rate; wet ingredients (meat, batter, eggs, preserves) read as damped squelches. No single prior covers both, so this must never be asserted from signal.
The same wet material is deliberately shared with GORE-SOURCE, and dry-pour rattle overlaps FOOD & DRINK-COOKING.

Metal cookware: a hard, fast attack followed by a LONG, strongly inharmonic ring. Pots, pans and big metal bowls are thin resonant shells, so decay_time_seconds_60db is the longest in FOOD & DRINK apart from stemware, and the partials are metallically detuned.
Ring time is the material axis across this whole category: pans ring long, glass rings bright and medium, ceramic plates tick and die, and food itself does not ring at all.

Signal-identical to METAL-IMPACT, METAL-HANDLE and METAL-MOVEMENT; only the kitchen context in the name places it here. Cutting-board and knife work will also drag toward WOOD-IMPACT and FOOD & DRINK-COOKING.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

explanation: Food and Drink sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: Fallback for food and drink sounds the siblings do not name. Assert only when no sibling matches by name.
Almost uninformative by construction: the category spans everything from a banquet ambience to a garnish being placed, so the priors are near-flat and text must carry the decision.

Sustained liquid flow: a stationary, unpitched, bright noise bed threaded with bubbles and glugs. High sustain_ratio, low crest_factor, high spectral_flatness, near-zero harmonicity — this is a noise process, not an event.

Filling a vessel has a real and pretty signature: as the liquid rises the air column above it SHORTENS, so its resonance climbs and spectral_centroid_slope_hz_per_second comes out positive over the pour. A free pour onto a surface does not do this, and no other FOOD & DRINK subcategory does it at all.
Acoustically the same as WATER-POUR. UCS splits them purely on whether the liquid is a consumption drink, which only the filename can say — hence narrowable, not separable.
FOOD & DRINK-DRINKING is the dark, damped, bodily counterpart; carbonation fizz after the pour will pull toward WATER-FIZZ.

explanation: in a restaurant or dinner at home. GLASSWARE it's own subcategory.

discriminators: Cutlery and crockery: very sharp, very bright, very high-crest ticks and clinks, usually several in a row as a place setting is handled or a drawer of silverware is moved.

Ring time is the material axis. Ceramic plates are internally damped and die fast; small metal cutlery rings only briefly because it is small. Both fall well short of the long ring of a big pan (FOOD & DRINK-KITCHENWARE) or the bright sustained ring of stemware (FOOD & DRINK-GLASSWARE). Short decay plus very high centroid is the tableware fingerprint.
Signal-identical to CERAMICS-IMPACT and METAL-IMPACT; the dining context in the name is what places it here.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)
